title: Early Intervention Programme for Infants & Children (EIPIC)
text:
Early Intervention Programme for Infants & Children (EIPIC) - SupportGoWhere
Early Intervention Programme for Infants & Children (EIPIC)
Share link
EIPIC is a centre-based programme that provides therapy and educational intervention for children aged 0-6 years who require medium to high levels of early intervention (EI) support.
SERVICE HIGHLIGHTS
Centre-based
Fees payable
Referral required
SERVICE PROVIDERS
Find service providers near you
Collapse all sections
About this support

EIPIC is a centre-based programme that provides therapy and educational intervention for children aged 0 to 6 years who require medium to high levels of early intervention (EI) support.

The continuum of EI services include:

EIPIC Under-2s

Targeted at children under 2 years old

Trains parents/caregivers so that they can effectively carry out intervention strategies in the child’s daily routines at home

EIPIC@Centre

Provides both therapy and educational intervention services, typically in small groups

DS-Plus

Provided in preschools targeted at children aged 2 to 6 years requiring low levels of EI support, and are attending preschools.

Who is this for?

Children who are:

From birth to 6 years old

Singapore Citizens or Permanent Residents;

Assessed by a paediatrician to be at risk of a developmental, intellectual, sensory or physical disability, or a combination of disabilities; and

Not concurrently enrolled in any other ECDA-funded EI programmes (i.e. DS-LS, DS-Plus programmes).

What to expect?

Upon receiving the referral, SG Enable will contact you within 10 working days to provide necessary information to help you make an informed decision on your choice of EI Centre.

Your selected EI Centre will contact you within 2 weeks of referral to arrange an interview and assessment of your child.

Enrolment into a suitable programme at the preferred centre is subject to the availability of vacancies at the centre and approval of the respective centre management.

How to apply?

You may approach a doctor at any polyclinic to obtain referral for assessment at the Department of Child Development (DCD) at KK Women’s and Children’s Hospital (KKH) or Child Development Unit (CDU) at National University Hospital (NUH).

Alternatively, you may approach a private paediatrician for assessment and recommendation for EIPIC.

If your child is assessed to be suitable for EIPIC, the hospital or the private paediatrician will recommend EIPIC and submit the application to SG Enable upon your consent.

More information can be found on the Enabling Guide.

Information last updated on 17 Sep 2025
